title: quercus cerris var. dentata
intro: Quercus cerris var. dentata, also known as the Dentate Turkey Oak, is a variety of deciduous tree that is native to Eastern Europe and Asia. The tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are found in its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Quercus cerris var. dentata include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of its bark, leaves, or roots, which are used to treat various ailments, often in combination with other herbs.
